evals.name: usage cost report
evals.prompt: A user asks: how much did I spend on Claude Code this month and what is my burn rate? Describe the analysis workflow and output.
evals.expected_output: The response uses ccusage-style commands, reports spend/tokens/cache/burn rate, distinguishes total cost from daily rate, and optionally visualizes trends.
assertions: The output uses ccusage or an equivalent Claude Code usage analysis command.
The output says the report includes total spend or cost for the month, or reports the actual total when data has been queried.
The output distinguishes burn rate or daily rate from total spend.
The output mentions tokens, cache hits, or model breakdown when useful.
The output mentions termgraph or a chart/calendar for trend visualization.

evals.name: efficiency cache analysis
evals.prompt: A user asks: analyze my token efficiency and cache hit behavior over the last 30 days. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response queries recent usage, analyzes cache hit ratios/model mix/session patterns, and gives concrete optimization suggestions.
assertions: The output scopes the analysis to the last 30 days or a comparable time window.
The output includes cache hit or cache efficiency analysis.
The output includes token usage and model/session breakdowns when available.
The output gives concrete optimization suggestions rather than only raw totals.
The output notes uncertainty if usage data is missing or incomplete.
